prompt: Explain the water cycle, in prose, without using any lists, bullets, headings, or numbered steps. Keep it to one paragraph.
response: Water begins its journey as vapor lifted from oceans, lakes, and damp earth by the warming touch of the sun, rising invisible into cooler air where it clusters into soft congregations of cloud that drift with the wind until their gathered weight succumbs to gravity and falls again as rain, snow, or gentle drizzle, soaking the skin of continents and seeping into soil to be taken up by roots or to slide through layers of rock until, gathered into streams and rivers, it returns once more to the open arms of the sea, restless still, ready to rise again.
